title: Arkansas History Scholar Path
subtitle: A pathway for learners who want deep historical context behind Arkansas civic life and public struggle.
description: A historical-intelligence track focused on Arkansas civic history, labor, race, backlash, institutions, and memory.
audience: Students, teachers, researchers, historians, public education leaders.

milestones.title: Arkansas Historical Core
milestones.description: Complete the arkansas historical core milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Begin with the central historical course.
Study labor and collective power in historical context.

milestones.title: History and Structure
milestones.description: Complete the history and structure milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Connect history to systems of representation.
Connect history to democratic design and ballot power.

milestones.title: Story, Memory, and Continuity
milestones.description: Complete the story, memory, and continuity milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Understand how pain becomes story and civic memory.
Think about how history is preserved and taught over time.
